# jjezs_paddock ₣BM

## Context

Regime enums in this project carry their values as bare English words —
`conjure`, `bind`, `graft` for `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`; `tether`, `airgap` for
`RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`; node-regime modes (`disabled`/`enabled`/`global`/
`allowlist`) for the `RBRN_*` family. These bare values appear scattered
across bash, AsciiDoc, Python (Cloud Build steps), and Rust (test
fixtures), with no cluster-discoverable marker tying any one value to its
enum family. This makes mode renames cross-language hand-search-and-replace
work, prone to silent drift.

There is one prior in-tree exemplar of the prefixed-value approach:
`bubep_linux | bubep_mac | bubep_windows` for `BURN_PLATFORM`. It works but
uses a different shape from what this heat adopts; harmonization is part of
the sweep.

The inventory pace has been completed (see heat log / slate commit
messages for depth). This paddock records the resolved scope and the
durable findings; line-numbered site lists deliberately live in the
discovery recipe, not here.

## MCM grounding — "sprue" is `mcm_sprue`, prefix discipline makes it an inlay

"Sprue" here is not a coined word — it is the MCM quoin `mcm_sprue`: *a
literal wire-level token representing a serialized name — the exact
character sequence as it appears in a wire format (a JSON property key, an
API field name, a protocol identifier).* Our regime enum values are
textbook sprues: `rbnve_conjure` is the verbatim token that appears in
`rbrv.env`, in the `_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE` Cloud Build substitution, in
build-info JSON, and in Python/shell comparisons.

MCM also states: *a sprue that follows the prefix naming discipline is also
an `mcm_inlay`* (prefix-recognized, not lexicon-catalogued). So precisely,
the values are *already* sprues even while bare; what this heat adds is the
prefix discipline that makes each sprue **also an inlay**. The spec pace
(₢BMAAA) should cite `mcm_sprue` + `mcm_inlay` for grounding rather than
inventing rationale.

Note on a sibling usage: ₣BK independently uses "sprue" for tabtarget
dispatch tokens (`{owner}ml_{launcher-id}`, e.g. `rbml_rbw`) and wrote a
local gloss in BCG/CLAUDE.md. That is *also* a valid `mcm_sprue` instance
(a literal dispatch-wire token) — not a competing definition, a sibling
one. Both heats correctly name wire-level literal tokens "sprue." ₣BK's BCG
text would ideally cite `mcm_sprue` rather than read as a freshly-minted
concept, but that is ₣BK's documentation concern, not a blocker here.

## Convention — locked

Pattern: `<proj><n><regime><e>_<value>` where:

- `<proj>` — project prefix (`rb` for Recipe Bottle, `bu` for BUK)
- `<n>`    — single letter signaling "this is a regime namespace value"
             (chosen `n` for "namespace", contrasting `r` which is taken
             for the regime-variable family `rbr*`/`bur*`)
- `<regime>` — single letter mirroring the regime family
             (`v` = vessel, `n` = node, `c` = config, `s` = station,
             `e` = environment, etc.)
- `<e>`    — single letter signaling "this is an enum value"
- `_<value>` — the canonical value name

Full target table (all in-scope enums):

| Variable                 | Regime | Bare → Sprue                                          |
|--------------------------|:------:|-------------------------------------------------------|
| RBRV_VESSEL_MODE         | v      | bind/conjure/graft → `rbnve_bind`/`_conjure`/`_graft` |
| RBRV_EGRESS_MODE         | v      | tether/airgap → `rbnve_tether`/`_airgap`              |
| RBRN_ENTRY_MODE          | n      | disabled/enabled → `rbnne_disabled`/`_enabled`        |
| RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE     | n      | disabled/global/allowlist → `rbnne_*`                 |
| RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE  | n      | disabled/global/allowlist → `rbnne_*`                 |
| BURN_PLATFORM            | n      | bubep_linux/mac/windows → `bunne_linux`/`_mac`/`_windows` |

Family-sharing is a deliberate property, not an accident: vessel-mode and
egress-mode share `rbnve_` (both vessel-regime); the three node-mode enums
share `rbnne_`, so `rbnne_disabled` is intentionally voiced by ENTRY,
DNS, and ACCESS alike. Uniqueness lives in the full quoin + the owning
variable, not in the 5-char prefix. A single grep per family returns the
whole cluster.

## Scope decisions — resolved

- **RBRV_VESSEL_MODE + RBRV_EGRESS_MODE** → `rbnve_*`. In.
- **RBRN_ENTRY_MODE / UPLINK_DNS_MODE / UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE** → `rbnne_*`.
  In. Their values are pure comparison tokens with no second life, so the
  prefix is free (no indirection needed).
- **BURN_PLATFORM** → harmonize `bubep_*` to `bunne_*` in the same sweep.
  Decided harmonize over grandfather, per load-bearing-complexity
  discipline — eliminates the two-shape wart rather than freezing it.
- **RBRN_RUNTIME (`docker`/`podman`) — EXCLUDED, stays bare.** Its value
  is consumed as the literal executable name (`command -v "${RBRN_RUNTIME}"`,
  `${RBRN_RUNTIME} image inspect`). Adopting the convention would require a
  permanent value→binary indirection layer at every dispatch site — a
  runtime cost paid for grep-discoverability of two values that
  `RBRN_RUNTIME` itself already marks. The convention would cost more than
  it returns. This is the one regime enum that does not earn the prefix;
  the exclusion is deliberate, not an oversight.
- **BURE_VERBOSE / BURE_COLOR** — out (bare ints, different problem class;
  unchanged from original framing).

## Comment / use-site classification — judgment work

Bare-word grep on a value yields four classes. The migration paces walk
the candidate list and classify each hit:

1. **Phase / concept references** — "the conjure phase", "the airgap
   build" — refer to the workflow, not the enum value. **Leave alone.**
2. **Enum-value references** — equality tests, case arms, JSON literals,
   `vessel mode is conjure`, defaults — the actual variable value.
   **Migrate.**
3. **Identifier embeds** — `RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc`, `rbho_conjure_*`,
   vessel sigils `rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap`, test-step labels like
   `ordain-conjure` — the bare word is part of an identifier. **Leave
   alone.**
4. **Coupled derived strings** — the value drives a derived token that
   lives in resource-name space and must stay bare even though it is
   semantically the enum value. **Leave the derived string bare; migrate
   only the dispatch that selects it.** Two confirmed witnesses:
   - egress → worker-pool suffixes (`RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_*`, the `-tether`/
     `-airgap` fragments in pool paths)
   - vessel-mode → hallmark prefixes (`RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_*` = `c`/`b`/`g`)
   Because of class 4, even a class-2 case arm is not blind find/replace:
   the arm becomes `rbnve_tether)` while the pool string it maps to stays
   `-tether`.

### Three judgment traps a naive grep misses

- **Default-value defaults** — `${z_mode:-conjure}` is class-2 but a
  `== "conjure"` grep skips it. The recipe must also grep `:-conjure`,
  `:-bind`, etc.
- **AsciiDoc wire-value tables read like prose** — a spec table cell
  documenting `(conjure, bind, or graft)` as a substitution value is
  class-2 (migrate), not class-1 prose. Easy to misclassify and leave
  stale.
- **Rust noise is almost all class-3** — the onboarding-scenario fixtures
  are full of step labels (`ordain-conjure`, `wrest-bind`) and sigil
  consts that stay bare. Only the `export RBRV_*_MODE="..."` baseline
  strings in the fast-fixture file actually migrate; that pace rebuilds
  the Rust crate (`tt/vow-b`).

### Noise ranking (classification effort)

Counterintuitively `conjure` is the noisiest value (heavy phase/operation
prose), then `bind` (English-word noise: bind-mount, IP bind), then
`graft`; `tether`/`airgap` are the cleanest (~10 sites each, mostly
class-2 or class-4). This is why egress is the cheap pilot and vessel-mode
is the heavy classification grind.

## Cloud Build detection gap — vessel-mode acceptance gate

Vessel-mode's value crosses a substitution boundary
(`_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE` / `_RBGV_VESSEL_MODE`) into a **remote execution
context** — Cloud Build step shells and Python steps compare against the
literal value. The local `regime-validation` net catches the
declaration↔Rust-fixture lockstep, but it does **not** exercise the
remote-side comparisons. A missed step-shell or Python site fails silently
until an actual conjure/graft cloud build runs.

**Consequence:** for the vessel-mode pace, "gauntlet green" is not
sufficient proof of done. Its acceptance gate is an actual **conjure +
graft cloud build** (the full ordain path), not just the fast/crucible
suites.

## Discovery recipe

- `grep -rn 'buv_enum_enroll' Tools/buk/ Tools/rbk/` — authoritative
  declarations; cross-walk `buv_gate_enroll` invocations to confirm no
  enum missed.
- Per value V: `grep -rn '\bV\b' Tools/ tt/` filtered by extension for
  code sites; also `grep -rn ':-V\b'` for default-value defaults.
- Comment/prose: `grep -rn '#.*\bV\b'` (bash/Python), `'//.*\bV\b'`
  (Rust), and scan `Tools/*/vov_veiled/*.adoc` for prose + wire-value
  tables.
- Substitution boundary: trace `_RBG[AV]_VESSEL_MODE` /
  `_RBG[AV]_EGRESS*` through `rbfd_*`/`rbfv_*` step assembly into the
  `rbgja*`/`rbgjv*` Python+shell steps.

## Posture

**Burn-bridges migration.** No backwards compatibility. Depots reform on
execution, vessels re-charge, regime files get edited in place. Operator
pre-flight: `rbw-MZ` before sweep, all crucibles quenched.

**Deep-test stragglers.** `regime-validation`, `dockerfile-hygiene`, and
`gauntlet` catch any local bare-value site, because the enum-validation
gate rejects a non-prefixed value once the declaration is updated. The
remote Cloud Build comparisons are the exception — see the detection gap
above.

## Heat-shape note — ₣BK relationship (BK coding landed)

₣BK (moorings-cutover) swept essentially this heat's file surface
(`Tools/rbk/**/*.sh`, `rbtd/src/*.rs`, `RBS*.adoc`) — but for path literals,
not enum values. The two heats are **semantically orthogonal** (different
concerns, mostly different lines). ₣BK's *coding* has landed (BKAAI
wrapped); the prior file-surface-churn concern is now historical — the tree
is settled and the re-validation confirmed the enum-value sites survived
intact.

What remains and matters:
- **Start gate:** ₣BK's two-platform gauntlet acceptance is still pending.
  Begin this heat only after ₣BK's gauntlet passes on linux + macos and any
  cutover bugfixes land — so a known-good cutover baseline exists and every
  ₣BM gauntlet signal is unambiguously about enums, not the cutover.
- **Residual churn risk:** only late ₣BK bugfixes from its gauntlet runs.
  Low; re-grep before editing if BK commits land meanwhile.
- The bubep→bunne harmonize touches BUK files (₣BK's other subtree). ₣BK's
  BUK code has landed, so no hard coordination remains — just awareness of
  any late bugfix churn there.

## What done looks like

- All in-scope bare enum values replaced with sprue-prefixed values across
  bash, AsciiDoc, Python (Cloud Build), and Rust; `RBRN_RUNTIME` left bare.
- `buv_enum_enroll` (and gated `buv_gate_enroll`) declarations updated to
  expect the new values; regime files (`rbrv.env`, `rbrn.env`, BURN
  profiles) updated in place.
- Class-4 derived strings (pool suffixes, hallmark prefixes) left bare;
  their selecting dispatch migrated.
- `regime-validation` + `dockerfile-hygiene` + `gauntlet` green.
- Vessel-mode additionally proven by a real conjure + graft cloud build.
- A single grep per family returns the complete cluster:
  `grep -rn rbnve_ Tools/`, `grep -rn rbnne_ Tools/`,
  `grep -rn bunne_ Tools/` — and `grep -rn bubep_` returns nothing.

## Out of scope

- Sprue convention applied to non-regime-enum domains (status codes, JSON
  wire-format keys, etc.) — separate heat.
- `RBRN_RUNTIME` (`docker`/`podman`) — executable-name coupling, excluded
  by decision above.
- `BURE_VERBOSE` / `BURE_COLOR` integer values — different problem class.
- Backwards-compatibility shims of any kind.

## Pace shape (deferred to mount-time)

Resolved cadence is per-variable-family, ordered cheap-pilot-first:

1. Convention spec authoring — formalize the convention as a spec section,
   citing `mcm_sprue` + `mcm_inlay` for grounding, plus the class-4
   "coupled derived string stays bare" rule (two witnesses) and the
   `RBRN_RUNTIME` exclusion rationale.
2. Egress-mode pilot — `rbnve_tether`/`_airgap`. Smallest, cleanest;
   proves the mechanical pattern and the class-4 distinction.
3. Vessel-mode — `rbnve_bind`/`_conjure`/`_graft`. Heaviest; crosses
   bash/adoc/Python(Cloud Build)/Rust. Acceptance gate = real conjure +
   graft cloud build.
4. Node-mode — `rbnne_*` for ENTRY/DNS/ACCESS. High English-word noise,
   heavy classification.
5. bubep → bunne harmonize — BUK files; last slice.
6. Final regression sweep — gauntlet end-to-end + per-family grep audit.

Slate as thin dockets at mount-time (depth in slate commit messages); do
not pre-cut detailed site lists from this paddock.

lastPrompt: I strongly prefer the 'rbw-tX' triggering tabtarget colophon to the 'rbtd-s' one.  What if we convert rbw-t to use the imprint and delete rbtd-s?  Do we lose any capability?

content.text:
Oriented. Here's where ₣BM stands and how this consolidation fits.

## Heat ₣BM — `rbk-09-mvp-regime-sprues-misc` (racing)

The heat's spine is the **regime-enum sprue migration** — replacing bare enum words (`conjure`/`tether`/`disabled`/…) with prefixed sprues (`rbnve_*`/`rbnne_*`/`bunne_*`). Most of that has landed; two paces remain, both `[rough]`:

- **₢BMAAH `cloud-depot-build-viability-fixture`** — the **dogfight** suite.
- **₢BMAAF `final-regression-and-grep-audit`** — the heat's gauntlet exit.

The colophon consolidation is "-misc" cleanup riding in this heat — thematically adjacent (both are naming-discipline sweeps), and you've chosen to house it here. Good.

## The dogfight interaction — this is the real reason for the ordering

₢BMAAH (dogfight) mints, per its docket:
- `ZRBTE_SUITE_DOGFIGHT` array + a `dogfight)` arm in `zrbte_resolve_suite`
- the tabtarget **`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.dogfight.sh`**

So dogfight writes a *new `rbtd-s` imprint and resolver arm* — exactly the surface the consolidation rewrites. The consequence is clean, not conflicting: **the consolidation absorbs `dogfight` as just one more `rbw-ts.TestSuite.<imprint>`.** The resolver array stays (it lives in `rbte_engine.sh`, which survives); only the colophon on the tabtarget and the dispatch path change. Net effect of dogfight on our pace: **+1 imprint to carry, and a hard "after AAH" ordering.** No rework.

## The ₢BMAAF interaction — forces consolidation to be the *last* pace

₢BMAAF's recipe hardcodes **`tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`** as the gauntlet runner. Our locked decision *deletes* `rbw-tP` (folded into `rbw-ts.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh`). So the consolidation must land **after** AAF — AAF runs its gauntlet under the old name and wraps; then consolidation renames everything and re-proves the gauntlet under the new name as *its own* acceptance gate. Ordering: **…→ AAH (dogfight) → AAF (final-regression) → consolidation (new, terminal).**

(One knock-on you should eyeball: `rbw-tP` is the documented release gate in CLAUDE.md and `CLAUDE.consumer.md`. Deleting it makes the release-gate identity `rbw-ts.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh` — a consumer-visible rename. That's inside the doc-sweep scope, but it's the most "load-bearing" rename in the set.)

## Candidate docket (draft — for refinement, not slating)

Locked decisions captured; tightened toward mechanical. Depth (the dogfight/AAF reasoning above) belongs in the slate commit message, not the body:

```
## Character
Mechanical rename + pipeline collapse. Judgment only at the colophon mint
and the doc-sweep's class-2-vs-prose calls. Strictly after dogfight (AAH)
and final-regression (AAF); terminal in ₣BM.

## Goal
Collapse the theurge rbtd-* tabtarget family into rbw-t* and retire the
second (RBTW) pipeline — all test/qualify dispatch on one workbench, no
test lost, materially fewer tabtargets.

## Locked shape
- Suites → rbw-ts.TestSuite.<imprint> (imprint channel), carrying every
  current imprint incl. dogfight + a folded `tadmor`.
- 17 rbtd-r.FixtureRun.* → ONE param colophon, full single-fixture run.
- Single case on charged crucible → rbw-tc.FixtureCase.sh <fixture> [case].
- Theurge crate build/test → rbw-tb / rbw-tt.
- Fold-and-delete rbw-tP / rbw-tS / rbw-tT (pure suite execs) into imprints.
- Keep rbw-tf (lint), rbw-tr (lint+shellcheck+complete), rbw-tK/tO.
- One pipeline: re-home rbte_cli as a buz module enrolled in rbz_zipper,
  dispatched by rbw_workbench; delete rbtw_workbench.sh,
  launcher.rbtw_workbench.sh, and the rbte_dispatch switch. rbte_engine.sh
  and the Rust binary unchanged.

## Recipe (not site lists)
- Live callers: grep -rn 'rbtd-[bstr]' Tools/ tt/ (exclude .claude/jjm).
- Operator-facing: the buh_tt calls naming rbtd-* in handbook scripts.
- Consumer-visible: CLAUDE.consumer.md ships these colophons — release-note.

## Acceptance
- grep -rn 'rbtd-' Tools/ tt/ returns zero live colophons (history aside).
- Gauntlet green via rbw-ts.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh (pre-flight rbw-MZ).
- Every prior suite + fixture reachable.
```

We're **not slating this now** — per your call, we refine to mechanical, then chivvy after dogfight. One genuine open item before it's mechanical: the FixtureRun colophon letter, because the natural mnemonics collide by case.

lastPrompt: I strongly prefer the 'rbw-tX' triggering tabtarget colophon to the 'rbtd-s' one.  What if we convert rbw-t to use the imprint and delete rbtd-s?  Do we lose any capability?

content.text:
Yes — a hard reason: **`rbw-tf` is already `QualifyFast`, and it's doubly load-bearing.** It's not just the standalone fast-lint command; `rbw_route` invokes `rbq_qualify_fast` as the **qualification gate before every `rbw-cC` (charge) and `rbw-fO` (ordain)** (`rbw_workbench.sh:64-67`). So `rbw-tf` is an occupied colophon with an active second role. Reusing it for FixtureRun would be a literal collision (same colophon, two meanings) — that breaks terminal exclusivity, not just the case-sensitivity rule. It's the one `rbw-t*` we're definitely keeping.

So `rbw-tf` is out, and `rbw-tF` is out (case-collides with it). After removing the forbidden letters (f, r, p, s, t, k, o — existing; plus c, b — newly minted), the clean candidates for FixtureRun are essentially `tx`, `tu`, or a few opaque others.

My recommendation: **`rbw-tx.FixtureRun.sh <fixture>`** (x = eXecute a fixture). It pairs cleanly with `rbw-tc.FixtureCase.sh <fixture> [case]` — `tx` runs the whole fixture, `tc` runs one case against an already-charged crucible. The readable distinction lives in the frontispiece (`FixtureRun` vs `FixtureCase`); the letters just need to be unique, and `tx`/`tc` are.

That closes the last open item. The full minted family would be:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Channel | Replaces |
|----------|-------------|---------|----------|
| `rbw-tb` | Build | — | `rbtd-b` |
| `rbw-tt` | Test | — | `rbtd-t` |
| `rbw-ts` | TestSuite.`<imprint>` | imprint | `rbtd-s.TestSuite.*` + folded `tP/tS/tT` |
| `rbw-tx` | FixtureRun | param1 | 17× `rbtd-r.FixtureRun.*` |
| `rbw-tc` | FixtureCase | param1 | `rbtd-s.FixtureCase` |
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | — | *(kept — lint + charge/ordain gate)* |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | — | *(kept — fix internal complete-suite call)* |
| `rbw-tK`/`rbw-tO` | Kludge/OrdainCycle | imprint | *(kept)* |

If `rbw-tx` sits right with you, the docket is mechanically complete — nothing left that requires mount-time judgment beyond the doc-sweep's prose-vs-value calls. We hold here until dogfight (₢BMAAH) and final-regression (₢BMAAF) land, then chivvy this as the terminal ₣BM pace. Want me to fold `rbw-tx` into the draft docket and park it, or leave it in this chat for you to slate when the time comes?

message.content: We could move 'qualify fast' to rbw-tq?

content.text:
Yes — and I think that's the better call. It gives the *natural* mnemonics to the test-execution verbs, which are now the core of this colophon family, and shunts Qualify (a peripheral gate concern) aside. `rbw-tf` = **F**ixtureRun pairs perfectly with `rbw-tc` = fixture**C**ase.

Two things confirm it's safe:

- **The charge/ordain gate is unaffected.** `rbw_route` invokes `rbq_qualify_fast` as a **function call** (`rbq_cli.sh rbq_qualify_fast`), not via the tabtarget colophon. Renaming the colophon `rbw-tf` → `rbw-tq` doesn't touch the gate at all.
- **`tq` is free** (no collision with tb/tt/ts/tc/tr/tK/tO or the now-freed tf), and QualifyFast's doc references get swept in the same pass we're already doing for the rbtd→rbw migration — marginal extra churn.

One small wrinkle worth naming so it's a conscious choice, not drift: the surviving Qualify pair becomes `tq` (Fast) + `tr` (Release), where `q` names the *category* and `r` names the *flavor* — mildly asymmetric. The clean-consistent alternative would be 3-letter `tqf`/`tqr`, but that breaks the well-established `tr` release-gate identity for no real gain. I'd keep `tq`/`tr` as-is.

Resulting minted family — now fully clean:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Channel | Replaces |
|----------|-------------|---------|----------|
| `rbw-tb` | Build | — | `rbtd-b` |
| `rbw-tt` | Test | — | `rbtd-t` |
| `rbw-ts` | TestSuite.`<imprint>` | imprint | `rbtd-s.TestSuite.*` + folded `tP/tS/tT` |
| `rbw-tf` | **FixtureRun** | param1 | 17× `rbtd-r.FixtureRun.*` |
| `rbw-tc` | FixtureCase | param1 | `rbtd-s.FixtureCase` |
| `rbw-tq` | **QualifyFast** *(moved from `tf`)* | — | — |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | — | *(kept — fix internal complete-suite call)* |
| `rbw-tK`/`rbw-tO` | Kludge/OrdainCycle | imprint | *(kept)* |

That closes the mint — nothing left in the docket requires mount-time judgment beyond the doc-sweep's prose-vs-value calls. I'll fold the `rbw-tf`/`rbw-tq` resolution into the draft and park it.

Per your standing call, I'm **not slating** — we hold until dogfight (₢BMAAH) and final-regression (₢BMAAF) land, then chivvy this as the terminal ₣BM pace (it must come after both: it absorbs dogfight's new imprint and renames the `rbw-tP` gate that AAF's recipe still calls).

The draft docket lives in this chat for when you're ready. Want me to add a `## Mint` block to it recording the full colophon table above (so the chivvy is copy-paste mechanical), or leave the docket as-is and keep the table here in conversation?

- `grep -rn 'rbtd-' Tools/ tt/` → zero live colophons (history aside)
- theurge crate builds clean; **`rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` green** — this alone proves the one-pipeline dispatch works end-to-end (rbw_workbench → buz_exec_lookup → re-homed rbte_cli → binary) on the cheap path
- **`rbw-tq.QualifyFast` green** — `buz_healthcheck` + tabtarget/colophon lint confirms every enrolled colophon resolves, catching any botched rename

**Step 2 — AAF (reslated, the long runs):** now the unified final regression. Its recipe gains:
- gauntlet via `rbw-ts.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh` (was `rbw-tP`)

This is better than my draft: the consolidation pace no longer carries a gauntlet line it can't cheaply satisfy, the expensive run happens exactly once, and AAF stays genuinely *final* — it regresses the whole settled tree. message.content: Does this fully remove the rbtd- colophon set?

content.text:
Yes — the **colophon set** is fully removed. All four `rbtd-` colophons remap:

| `rbtd-` colophon | → | New |
|---|---|---|
| `rbtd-b` (Build) | → | `rbw-tb` |
| `rbtd-t` (Test) | → | `rbw-tt` |
| `rbtd-r` (FixtureRun ×17) | → | `rbw-tf` |
| `rbtd-s` (TestSuite *and* FixtureCase) | → | `rbw-ts` + `rbw-tc` |

That's the complete set — `rbtd-s` is the only one carrying two frontispieces, and both are covered. So after AAK, no `rbtd-` *colophon* survives.

Two precisions on what "fully remove" does **not** mean, so the AAF audit doesn't trip:

1. **`rbtd` survives as a primary-universe prefix — intentionally.** The crate directory `Tools/rbk/rbtd/`, the binary `target/debug/rbtd`, and every `rbtd*/rbte*` source file (`rbte_engine.sh`, `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, …) keep their names. This consolidation retires the *tabtarget colophons*, not the theurge codebase. The `RBTW` acronym (→ `rbtw_workbench.sh`) *does* go dangling when that file is deleted — the doc sweep must drop/repoint it.

2. **`grep -rn 'rbtd-' Tools/` will not return literally zero lines.** There's a legitimate non-colophon survivor: the scratch-dir prefix `format!("rbtd-test-{}-{}", …)` in `rbtdte_engine.rs:44` and `rbtdti_invocation.rs:29`. AAF's audit line says "zero live *colophons*," which is correct in intent — but the raw grep returns those two `rbtd-test-` hits, and the auditor has to classify them as non-colophon (the same prose-vs-value discipline the sprue migration already uses).

That second point is a foreseeable false-positive at AAF mount-time. Want me to tighten AAF's audit line to name the `rbtd-test-` scratch prefix as the one expected survivor — so the gauntlet-exit agent doesn't pause on it? It's a one-line reslate and a durable fact (a code identifier, not a count).

lastPrompt: Does this fully remove the rbtd- colophon set?

Disk survey (threshold 85%): 5.4 TB: 12%

Created: 260514


## Paces

### theurge-colophon-consolidation (₢BMAAK) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical rename + single-pipeline collapse. Judgment only at the
doc-sweep's prose-vs-value calls; the colophon mint is pre-resolved below.
Intricate but mechanical — hands the heavy gauntlet/cloud regression to the
final-regression pace that follows.

## Goal
Collapse the theurge rbtd-* tabtarget family into rbw-t* and retire the
second (RBTW) pipeline — all test/qualify dispatch on one workbench, no
test lost, materially fewer tabtargets.

## Mint (resolved)
- rbw-tb Build           <- rbtd-b
- rbw-tt Test            <- rbtd-t
- rbw-ts TestSuite.<imprint> (imprint)  <- rbtd-s.TestSuite.* + folded
  tP/tS/tT; carries every current imprint incl. dogfight + new `tadmor`.
- rbw-tf FixtureRun (param1)            <- 17x rbtd-r.FixtureRun.*
- rbw-tc FixtureCase <fixture> [case] (param1)  <- rbtd-s.FixtureCase
- rbw-tq QualifyFast (moved off rbw-tf); rbw-tr QualifyRelease kept (fix
  its complete-suite call); rbw-tK/tO kept.

## Shape — one pipeline
Re-home rbte_cli as a buz module enrolled in rbz_zipper, dispatched by
rbw_workbench. Delete rbtw_workbench.sh, launcher.rbtw_workbench.sh, and
the rbte_dispatch switch. rbte_engine.sh and the Rust binary unchanged.

## Recipe (not site lists)
- Live callers: grep -rn 'rbtd-[bstr]' Tools/ tt/ (exclude .claude/jjm).
- Operator-facing: buh_tt calls naming rbtd-* in handbook scripts.
- Consumer-visible: CLAUDE.consumer.md ships these colophons — release-note
  the rename (esp. rbw-tP release-gate -> rbw-ts.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh).

## Done (cheap/local — heavy regression is the next pace, AAF)
- grep -rn 'rbtd-' Tools/ tt/ returns zero live colophons (history aside).
- Theurge crate builds clean; rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh green (proves the
  one-pipeline dispatch end-to-end on the cheap path).
- rbw-tq QualifyFast green (colophon/tabtarget health — every enrolled
  colophon resolves).

### final-regression-and-grep-audit (₢BMAAF) [rough]

## Character

Gauntlet exit. A failure here is a real migration bug (a missed site), not
exploration — triage is opus-grade.

## Goal

The whole sprue migration AND the theurge-colophon consolidation proven
end-to-end, and the discoverability promise verified: one grep per family
returns the complete cluster — no bare enum values and no stale rbtd-
colophons survive.

## Recipe

- Run the gauntlet (`tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh`) — pre-flight `rbw-MZ`,
  all crucibles quenched, per burn-bridges posture.
- Grep audit:
  - `grep -rn rbnve_ Tools/` / `rbnne_` / `bunne_` each return a complete,
    coherent cluster.
  - `grep -rn bubep_` returns nothing.
  - `grep -rn 'rbtd-' Tools/ tt/` returns zero live colophons (.claude/jjm
    history aside).
  - Spot bare-value audit: surviving `conjure`/`graft`/`tether`/`airgap`/
    `disabled`/`enabled`/`global`/`allowlist` hits are all class-1/3/4
    (prose, identifiers, coupled derived strings), zero class-2.

## Acceptance

- Gauntlet green (via the new rbw-ts.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh name).
- Grep audit clean per above.

=== QualifyTadmor body (what 'tadmor suite' must absorb) ===
rbq_qualify_tadmor() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  # Tadmor self-contained — fully local, no GCP/depot/project. Two fixtures in
  # sequence: kludge-tadmor builds BOTH vessels (sentry + bottle) locally and
  # commits each hallmark (the fixture owns the notch — same precedent as
  # onboarding's rbtdro_kludge_nameplate); then the tadmor crucible fixture
  # charges against the now-clean nameplate, runs the security cases, quenches.
  # The build is a separate fixture (nameplate passed explicitly) rather than a
  # self-charging tadmor fixture, because the crucible security cases resolve
  # their nameplate from the fixture name and would collide on "tadmor".
  buc_step "Building tadmor vessels locally (kludge sentry + bottle, commit hallmarks)"
  "${ZRBQ_PROJECT_ROOT}/tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.kludge-tadmor.sh"

  buc_step "Running tadmor crucible fixture (charge + security + quench)"
  "${ZRBQ_PROJECT_ROOT}/tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.tadmor.sh"

=== is rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md generated, and by what? ===
Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh
Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh

content.text:
Reviewed both dockets against the live code. Found one real **gap that makes AAK's own Done criterion unreachable as written**, two **inaccuracies**, and two **capability-preservation omissions**. Details, grounded in what I just verified:

## Must-fix

**1. AAK Done is unreachable without a tabtarget-context regen step (the important one).**
`rbq_qualify_fast` calls `rbq_qualify_context` (`rbq_Qualify.sh:162`), which **hard-dies if `rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md` is stale** vs the zipper registry (`:141`, `:145` — "run tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh"). Enrolling the new `rbw-t*` colophons in `rbz_zipper` *makes it stale by definition*. So AAK's Done line "rbw-tq QualifyFast green" cannot pass until the agent runs `rbw-MG` to regenerate it. It's self-documenting (the die message names the fix), but the recipe should say so — and note the regen also refreshes the `@`-included Command Reference. **Add to recipe: "regenerate the generated tabtarget context via `rbw-MG` (QualifyFast's staleness gate fails otherwise)."**

**2. "rbw-tr QualifyRelease kept (fix its complete-suite call)" is inaccurate — and it's good news.**
The line already reads `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.complete.sh` (`rbq_Qualify.sh:184`). That's the "rot" we found earlier — but it's actually a *forward reference*: it points at the name suites get **in this pace**. So there's nothing to fix; **AAK auto-resolves the broken reference by creating `rbw-ts.TestSuite.complete.sh`.** Reframe: "its existing `rbw-ts.TestSuite.complete.sh` call becomes valid once suites land at `rbw-ts` — this pace closes the long-standing dangling reference."

**3. Count error: "17× rbtd-r.FixtureRun.*" — there are 16** (verified: `ls | wc -l` = 16; dogfight has no FixtureRun tabtarget). Per the stale-by-mount rule, exact counts shouldn't be in dockets anyway. **Drop the number: "every `rbtd-r.FixtureRun.*` tabtarget."**

## Should-fix (capability preservation — the "no test lost" promise)

**4. "new `tadmor`" suite must be `(kludge-tadmor, tadmor)`, in order — not just `(tadmor)`.** `rbq_qualify_tadmor` deliberately runs the local kludge build *then* the crucible fixture as two separate fixtures (the comment at `rbq_Qualify.sh:225-228` explains they can't merge — nameplate collision). A naive fold to a single-fixture `tadmor` suite silently drops the local vessel build. **One clause: "tadmor suite = (kludge-tadmor, tadmor), preserving QualifyTadmor's two-fixture sequence."**

**5. FixtureCase's omit-to-list behavior isn't flagged.** The `param1` collapse must preserve: no fixture → list fixtures; fixture only → list cases (the crucible-debug loop documented in CLAUDE.md and the theurge-ifrit context). It's the trickiest part of the `param1` rework, and easy to lose. **One clause: "preserve FixtureCase omit-to-list listing."**

## Notes (no edit needed)
- The `param1` `rbw-tf` FixtureRun *gains* dogfight coverage for free (the binary knows the fixture; the 16 imprints never included it) — a small win, not a problem.
- Minor execution-order point a mechanical agent handles: move QualifyFast `tf→tq` *before* repurposing `tf` for FixtureRun, to avoid a transient same-colophon collision.
- The `rbtd-test-` scratch-prefix false-positive in AAF's grep audit (raised last turn) is still open — worth folding the fix into this same reslate.

If you agree, I'll do a single `jjx_redocket` mass-reslate of **both** AAK (items 1–5) and AAF (item: name `rbtd-test-` as the one expected non-colophon grep survivor). Want me to apply all of these, or drop/adjust any first?

message.content: Apply all now please.

content.text: I'll mass-reslate both paces in one `jjx_redocket`. Writing the gazette with both notices.

final-regression pace that follows.

The order stands: **₢BMAAK → ₢BMAAF**, both the only un-wrapped paces in ₣BM. Dockets are now mechanically clean and self-consistent against the live code — ready to mount ₢BMAAK in a fresh chat whenever you are.

No — the gauntlet is **not** in ₢BMAAK. That's exactly the split you carved out two turns ago. AAK's Done is deliberately cheap/local, no cloud and no crucibles:

- `grep -rn 'rbtd-' Tools/ tt/` → zero live colophons
- theurge crate builds clean; **`rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` green** (the fast tier — enrollment/regime/handbook/hygiene, no dependencies, sub-minute)
- **`rbw-tq QualifyFast` green** (static lint, after the `rbw-MG` regen)

All of that runs in minutes. The ~2-hour gauntlet (`rbw-MZ` pre-flight + cloud builds + crucible charges) lives entirely in **₢BMAAF**, the next pace — its docket header even reads "heavy regression is the next pace, AAF."

message.content:
Help me write an answer to that other chat, it seems confused about this:


⏺ The param1 path works end-to-end: FixtureCase with no arg reaches rbte_single, which forwards to the binary's single
  mode and lists fixtures. So the dispatch rework did not break folio sourcing. Here's what I've found.

  Orientation

  Heat ₣BM — rbk-09-mvp-regime-sprues-misc (9/10 done). The enum-sprue migration: bare regime enum values
  (conjure/tether/disabled/…) replaced with prefix-discoverable sprues (rbnve_*, rbnne_*, bunne_*).

  Next pace ₢BMAAF (final-regression-and-grep-audit) — the gauntlet exit. Goal: prove the whole migration end-to-end
  (tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh green) and verify the discoverability promise (one grep per family returns the complete
  cluster; bubep_ returns nothing; surviving bare-value hits are all class-1/3/4).

  The admonition, decoded against the code

  Your admonition is about test-engine folio sourcing — and it is not in ₢BMAAF's docket (that pace is purely the enum
  gauntlet + grep audit). It reads as a deferred decision from ₢BMAAJ (tabtarget-dispatch-via-burd-launcher), which
  landed without resolving it. Here's the actual state:

  - Canonical BUK mechanism (buz_exec_lookup, buz_zipper.sh:258-280): a zipper enrollment declares each colophon's
  channel; the dispatcher decodes the folio (imprint→BURD_TOKEN_3, param1→first positional) and execs with
  BUZ_FOLIO=<folio>. The command reads BUZ_FOLIO, channel-agnostic.
  - Theurge does not use this. rbz_zipper.sh is empty. rbte_dispatch hand-routes on BURD_TOKEN_1/2, and:
    - rbte_run / rbte_suite read BURD_TOKEN_3 directly (imprint).
    - rbte_single reads positional $1/$2 (fixture + case).
    - The zbuz_kindle/zrbz_kindle in rbte_cli.sh are vestigial against the empty zipper.

  Why it hand-parses (the real friction): the canonical mechanism keys channel by colophon and yields one BUZ_FOLIO. But
  rbtd-s carries two frontispieces with two different channels under one colophon — TestSuite (imprint, one folio) and
  FixtureCase (param1, two values: fixture + case). Plus rbtd-b/rbtd-t take no folio but pass cargo args through. The
  single-channel-per-colophon, single-folio model can't represent that without splitting colophons — and even then,
  FixtureCase's two-valued need exceeds one BUZ_FOLIO.

  Empirically: folio sourcing works today (just demonstrated). Nothing is broken by the dispatch rework. So the
  "decision" is purely architectural convergence, not a repair.

  My read: full BUZ_FOLIO convergence is a poor fit here and a larger refactor that belongs to test-infra work (₣BJ
  territory), not a precondition smuggled into the enum-sprue gauntlet. But I want your call before I touch anything or
  run the gauntlet.

⏺ User declined to answer questions
  ⎿  · How do you want to resolve the rbte folio-sourcing admonition before running the ₢BMAAF gauntlet? (Keep bespoke +
     note why / Converge to BUZ_FOLIO now / Carve separate pace, proceed now)

❯ Can you state the question more clearly?  Your prose is too dense for me right now.

Here's the situation: that chat is **out of date and in the wrong pace**. It's treating the theurge folio-sourcing thing as a loose end blocking the gauntlet — but that's not a loose end at all. It's the *entire scope* of a pace that was slated after that chat last oriented: **₢BMAAK (theurge-colophon-consolidation)**, which is ordered **before** ₢BMAAF.

Two tells that it's stale: it never mentions ₢BMAAK, and its AAF goal still says `rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh` — but AAF was reslated to run `rbw-ts.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh`. So it's holding a pre-reslate view.

Here's a paste-ready reply you can drop into that chat:

---

You've rediscovered the exact scope of a pace that was slated after you last oriented — your view is stale. Re-orient before doing anything: `jjx_orient` on ₣BM (make sure you're on the latest commit first).

What you found is real and correct: theurge hand-routes via `rbte_dispatch` on `BURD_TOKEN_*`, `rbz_zipper.sh` is empty, the `zbuz_kindle`/`zrbz_kindle` calls are vestigial, and `rbte_single`/`rbte_run` source the folio the old way instead of via `BUZ_FOLIO`. **That is precisely the defined work of pace ₢BMAAK (theurge-colophon-consolidation)** — re-home `rbte_cli` as a buz module enrolled in `rbz_zipper`, dispatch through `rbw_workbench`, delete `rbtw_workbench.sh` + its launcher + the `rbte_dispatch` switch. The BUZ_FOLIO convergence you're contemplating isn't an ad-hoc decision and isn't a future task to file — it's already a slated pace.

So, concretely:

1. **You're in the wrong pace.** ₢BMAAK comes **before** ₢BMAAF. AAF is not next; AAK is. Don't run the gauntlet from here.
2. **Don't touch folio sourcing under AAF, and don't note it as a new task.** It's neither broken (you proved it works) nor in AAF's scope. For AAF's own purposes the answer is "leave it" — but really you just shouldn't be in AAF yet.
3. **AAF can't even run yet.** Its reslated recipe calls `rbw-ts.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh`, which doesn't exist until AAK lands (today it's still `rbw-tP`). Its grep audit was also extended to assert no stale `rbtd-` colophons survive. Both only make sense after AAK.

Re-orient, mount ₢BMAAK, and the "admonition" resolves itself — it's the body of that pace, not a precondition to the gauntlet.

---

One note for *you* (not the other chat): the two-frontispiece concern that agent raised — `rbtd-s` carrying both TestSuite (imprint) and FixtureCase (param1) under one colophon — is already resolved in AAK's mint by splitting them into `rbw-ts` (pure imprint) and `rbw-tc` (pure param1). And the "two values exceed one BUZ_FOLIO" worry is unfounded: the `param1` channel puts the fixture in `BUZ_FOLIO` and passes the remaining positional (the case) straight through. If anything genuinely needs care in AAK it's the *no-folio* channel for `rbw-tb`/`rbw-tt` (Build/Test) — the empty channel in `buz_exec_lookup` *drops* extra args, and those two pass cargo args through. That's an AAK mount-time detail, not anything the AAF chat needs to worry about.

content.text:
Here's a paste-ready prompt for a fresh chat. It forces orientation and self-verification *before* any edits, pins the right pace, and walls off the gauntlet — the three things that tripped the other chat.

---

```
Mount and execute pace ₢BMAAK (theurge-colophon-consolidation) in heat ₣BM.

STEP 0 — Self-checks. Run these and REPORT the results before touching any
file. If ANY check fails, STOP and tell me — do not improvise.

  a. git: confirm HEAD includes the AAK slate + AAF reslate work. Run
     `git log --oneline -5` and `git status`. If the working tree is dirty
     with someone else's changes, stop and ask.
  b. Open an officium, then `jjx_orient` on ₣BM.
  c. Assert remaining-pace order is exactly [₢BMAAK, ₢BMAAF] (jjx_coronets
     BM remaining). ₢BMAAK MUST be the next actionable pace. If ₢BMAAF
     appears first, or ₢BMAAK is missing, you are on a stale view — STOP.
  d. Read ₢BMAAK's docket from the gazette. Confirm its Goal is "Collapse
     the theurge rbtd-* tabtarget family into rbw-t*". If the docket you see
     is about the enum-sprue gauntlet, you mounted the wrong pace — STOP.

Only after all four pass: lead with the pace goal in one sentence, show me
the mint table from the docket, and propose your approach. Then begin.

SCOPE WALL — this is the CHEAP/LOCAL pace. Do NOT:
  - run the gauntlet (rbw-tP, or rbw-ts.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh)
  - run any cloud build, depot levy, or crucible charge
  - touch ₢BMAAF or its docket
The heavy ~2h regression belongs to ₢BMAAF, the NEXT pace. Your only
verification is the docket's Done: build clean + rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh
green + rbw-tq QualifyFast green (after rbw-MG regen) + grep shows zero live
rbtd- colophons.

KNOWN SHARP EDGES — these are THIS pace's body, not blockers or deferrals.
Resolve each against the actual code; do not treat any as out-of-scope:
  - Theurge currently hand-routes (rbte_dispatch on BURD_TOKEN_*, empty
    rbz_zipper, vestigial zbuz/zrbz kindle). Converging it to the canonical
    buz_exec_lookup / BUZ_FOLIO mechanism, enrolled in rbz_zipper and
    dispatched by rbw_workbench, IS the work of this pace. It is not broken
    and not a separate decision — it is what you are here to do.
  - Channel split dissolves the old two-frontispiece friction: rbw-ts is
    pure imprint; rbw-tc is param1 with fixture in BUZ_FOLIO and the case
    passed through as the remaining positional; rbw-tf is param1 (fixture).
  - Trap: the no-folio ("") channel in buz_exec_lookup DROPS extra args.
    rbw-tb/rbw-tt (Build/Test) pass cargo args through — verify they still
    forward args after enrollment; the empty channel naively will not.
  - Order: rename QualifyFast tf→tq BEFORE repurposing tf for FixtureRun,
    or you get a transient same-colophon collision.
  - rbw-MG regen of the generated tabtarget context is required, or
    rbq_qualify_context (inside QualifyFast) hard-fails on staleness.

Follow the CLAUDE.md JJK mount protocol (read paddock before docket). Notch
with jjx_record as you go; do NOT wrap — ask me first.
```

---

A couple of things I deliberately built in, given what just happened:

- **Check (c) is the real safety latch.** The other chat thought ₢BMAAF was next because it never saw ₢BMAAK. Asserting the `[₢BMAAK, ₢BMAAF]` order forces a fresh read of committed gallops state and catches a stale clone before any work.
- **The "known sharp edges" framing as *body, not blocker*** is the specific antidote to the other chat's failure — it discovered the hand-dispatch fact and froze, asking whether to converge. This prompt tells the agent up front that converging *is the job*.
- I left the actual folio-sourcing implementation unprescribed (per docket discipline) — the prompt says "resolve against the actual code," which is the mount-time judgment that belongs to the agent, not pre-baked here.

Want me to also add an explicit line telling it to re-`git pull` first (in case the fresh chat starts on a clone behind `22eeba86b`), or are you running it from an up-to-date tree?
